source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XV. 1–3.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This passage of the Gospel, brethren, where the Lord calls Himself the vine, and His disciples the branches, declares in so many words that the Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, is the head of the Church, and that we are His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as the vine and its branches are of one nature, therefore, His own nature as God being different from ours, He became man, that in Him human nature might be the vine, and we who also are men might become branches thereof.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What mean, then, the words, “I am the true vine”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it to the literal vine, from which that metaphor was drawn, that He intended to point them by the addition of “true”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is by similitude, and not by any personal propriety, that He is thus called a vine; just as He is also termed a sheep, a lamb, a lion, a rock, a corner-stone, and other names of a like kind, which are themselves rather the true ones, from which these are drawn as similitudes, not as realities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He says, “I am the true vine,” it is to distinguish Himself, doubtless, from that [vine] to which the words are addressed: “How art thou turned into sourness, as a strange vine?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could that be a true vine which was expected to bring forth grapes and brought forth thorns?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am,” He says, “the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit, He taketh away; and every one that beareth fruit, He purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are, then, the husbandman and the vine one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is the vine in the same sense as when He said, “The Father is greater than I;” but in that sense wherein He said, “I and my Father are one,” He is also the husbandman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet not such a one as those, whose whole service is confined to external labor; but such, that He also supplies the increase from within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For neither is he that planteth anything, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ is certainly God, for the Word was God; and so He and the Father are one: and if the Word was made flesh,—that which He was not before,—He nevertheless still remains what He was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And still more, after saying of the Father, as of the husbandman, that He taketh away the fruitless branches, and pruneth the fruitful, that they may bring forth more fruit, He straightway points to Himself as also the purger of the branches, when He says, “Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, you see, He is also the pruner of the branches—a work which belongs to the husbandman, and not to the vine; and more than that, He maketh the branches His workmen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although they give not the increase, they afford some help; but not of themselves: “For without me,” He says, “ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And listen, also, to their own confession: “What, then, is Apollos, and what is Paul? but ministers by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have planted, Apollos watered.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this, too, “as the Lord gave to every man;” and so not of themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that, however, which follows, “but God gave the increase,” He works not by them, but by Himself; for work like that exceeds the lowly capacity of man, transcends the lofty powers of angels, and rests solely and entirely in the hands of the Triune Husbandman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now ye are clean,” that is, clean, and yet still further to be cleansed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, had they not been clean, they could not have borne fruit; and yet every one that beareth fruit is purged by the husbandman, that he may bring forth more fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He bears fruit because he is clean; and to bear more, he is cleansed still further.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who in this life is so clean as not to be in need of still further and further cleansing? seeing that, “if we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us; but if we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness;” to cleanse in very deed the clean, that is, the fruitful, that they may be so much the more fruitful, as they have been made the cleaner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does He not say, Ye are clean through the baptism wherewith ye have been washed, but “through the word which I have spoken unto you,” save only that in the water also it is the word that cleanseth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take away the word, and the water is neither more nor less than water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The word is added to the element, and there results the Sacrament, as if itself also a kind of visible word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had said also to the same effect, when washing the disciples’ feet, “He that is washed needeth not, save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence has water so great an efficacy, as in touching the body to cleanse the soul, save by the operation of the word; and that not because it is uttered, but because it is believed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even in the word itself the passing sound is one thing, the abiding efficacy another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This is the word of faith which we preach,” says the apostle, “that if thou shalt confess with thy mouth that Jesus is the Lord, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness, and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, we read in the Acts of the Apostles, “Purifying their hearts by faith;” and, says the blessed Peter in his epistle, “Even as baptism doth also now save us, not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This is the word of faith which we preach,” whereby baptism, doubtless, is also consecrated, in order to its possession of the power to cleanse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ, who is the vine with us, and the husbandman with the Father, “loved the Church, and gave Himself for it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then read the apostle, and see what he adds: “That He might sanctify it, cleansing it with the washing of water by the word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The cleansing, therefore, would on no account be attributed to the fleeting and perishable element, were it not for that which is added, “by the word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This word of faith possesses such virtue in the Church of God, that through the medium of him who in faith presents, and blesses, and sprinkles it, He cleanseth even the tiny infant, although itself unable as yet with the heart to believe unto righteousness, and to make confession with the mouth unto salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this is done by means of the word, whereof the Lord saith, “Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.”
